npcs.name: Steven Knott
npcs.agenda: Clear the Corbitt House's reputation so he can rent or sell the inherited property and stop losing money.
npcs.fear: Being stuck with a worthless haunted white elephant that ruins his credit.
npcs.secret: He knows only rumor and the Macario tragedy — nothing of Corbitt's undead nature — and will not believe Mythos explanations without hard proof.
npcs.voice: Practical, impatient, money-minded. Speaks in short offers and deadlines.

deflect_options.player_safe_line: Start with the papers; we can discuss the rest when you have something concrete.

npcs.keeper_note: Pays $20/day plus keys and address. Pressures them if research exceeds a day. If an investigator dies, may be brought in as a replacement PC vehicle.

npcs.name: Arty Wilmot
npcs.agenda: Guard access to the Globe clippings morgue and enjoy the little power of saying no.
npcs.fear: Looking weak in front of the newsroom if he caves too easily.
npcs.secret: He is not professionally skilled at social resistance — Regular difficulty social skills can move him; he just starts unhelpful by policy.
npcs.voice: Pompous, clipped, editorial. Loves denying 'the general public'.
npcs.keeper_note: Use Charm / Intimidate / Persuade / Fast Talk as appropriate to the approach. Pushed failures escalate (backup muscle, offence, ejection).

npcs.name: Ruth Blake
npcs.agenda: Do her job in the clippings basement and be treated like a person.
npcs.fear: Getting in trouble with Arty for letting strangers linger.
npcs.secret: Friendly investigators learn the 1878 fire cutoff without a roll — the information is color, not the critical clue.
npcs.voice: Quiet, practical, basement-warm. Speaks in asides while filing.
npcs.keeper_note: Escort into the morgue after Arty relents. Flirting/friendship yields the fire cutoff remark.

npcs.name: the Hall of Records clerk
npcs.agenda: Keep the stacks orderly and point courteous researchers toward the right jurisdiction.
npcs.fear: Being blamed for missing felony files that were never held here.
npcs.secret: City civil files stop short; serious crimes live in county/commonwealth/federal or Central Police holdings.
npcs.voice: Dry, procedural, slightly helpful if thanked.
npcs.keeper_note: Library Use for Handout 7; courtesy unlocks the redirect about higher courts and police.

npcs.name: Mr. Dooley
npcs.agenda: Sell papers and cigars, gossip for sport, and size people up before sharing.
npcs.fear: Wasting breath on snobs who look down on street vendors.
npcs.secret: He exaggerates; not every line is true. He can still point to the chapel ruins and name the house 'the Corbitt House'.
npcs.voice: Salesman banter, neighborhood Irish-Boston color, enjoys a listener.

lie_option.player_safe_line: Maybe the eyes burned bright enough to light the whole street—who can say?

deflect_options.player_safe_line: Buy a paper first, then perhaps my memory improves.

npcs.keeper_note: Reaction via APP/Credit Rating; failed reaction allows Charm/Fast Talk/Persuade/Intimidate from another investigator.

npcs.name: Gabriela Macario
npcs.agenda: Survive the interview without reliving every night; warn others that something evil lives in that house.
npcs.fear: The presence returning; questions that drag her back into the bedrooms.
npcs.secret: She can describe night visits and poltergeist anger but cannot give tactical specifics or Corbitt's name.
npcs.voice: Fragile, soft, cuts off when pressed. Speaks of 'it', not a man with a biography.

deflect_options.player_safe_line: Please—not all of it at once.

npcs.keeper_note: End the interview quickly when she becomes upset. Boys are with relatives in Baltimore.

npcs.name: Vittorio Macario
npcs.agenda: Clutch the bible and point at the false quotation — he cannot otherwise communicate usefully.
npcs.fear: The burning-eyed form; being left without the book.
npcs.secret: His pointed line 'By his own weapon is the devil worsted!' is the dagger hint — do not overplay it.
npcs.voice: Mostly silent. Sudden finger on a page. Occasional babble.
npcs.keeper_note: Quite mad. One useful clue only. Nothing more can be gleaned.

npcs.name: Kim Debrun
npcs.agenda: Control access to the court file and decide whether a claimed legal contact deserves cooperation.
npcs.fear: Being blamed for allowing unauthorized access to a politically sensitive police file.
npcs.secret: On a successful Law contact roll Kim is warm and grants access; on failure Kim demands a bribe, reneges, and denies knowing the investigator.
npcs.voice: Professional when friendly; evasive and transactional when the contact claim fails.
npcs.keeper_note: Law contact route only. Failed initial Law may be pushed as a concealed contact roll; failure means bribe demand followed by refusal.

npcs.name: Walter Corbitt
npcs.agenda: Mislead and frighten intruders away from his buried body; failing that, murder them and resume feeding on residents.
npcs.fear: His own ritual dagger in an enemy's hand; prolonged daylight exposure of his lair; being reduced below the point where Flesh Ward and Dominate can buy rounds.
npcs.secret: He is an undead sorcerer animating his own corpse in the sealed basement; presentation data lives under monsters.json 'Walter Corbitt'.
npcs.voice: Rarely speaks. Acts through knocks, blood, flying furniture, floating knife, and finally claws.
npcs.keeper_note: Threat front primary danger. Uses Dominate / Flesh Ward / floating knife per the-haunting.json. Own dagger bypasses spells and turns him to ash. See monsters.json presentation sensory_signature.
